# pfsense_firewall_schedule (Resource)

[Firewall schedule](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/firewall/time-based-rules.html) defining time ranges during which firewall rules referencing it are active.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pfsense_firewall_schedule" "business_hours" {
  name        = "BusinessHours"
  description = "Weekdays 9am to 5pm"

  time_range = [
    {
      position          = "1,2,3,4,5"
      start_time        = "9:00"
      stop_time         = "17:00"
      range_description = "Monday through Friday"
    },
  ]
}

resource "pfsense_firewall_schedule" "holidays" {
  name        = "Holidays"
  description = "Specific calendar days"

  time_range = [
    {
      month             = "12"
      day               = "25"
      start_time        = "0:00"
      stop_time         = "23:59"
      range_description = "Christmas Day"
    },
    {
      month             = "1"
      day               = "1"
      start_time        = "0:00"
      stop_time         = "23:59"
      range_description = "New Year's Day"
    },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the schedule, used as the unique identifier. May only consist of the characters a-z, A-Z, 0-9 and _, and may not be 'LAN' or 'WAN'.
- `time_range` (Attributes List) List of time ranges during which the schedule is active. At least one is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--time_range))

### Optional

- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).

### Read-Only

- `label` (String) Internal label automatically generated by pfSense to identify the schedule.

<a id="nestedatt--time_range"></a>
### Nested Schema for `time_range`

Required:

- `start_time` (String) Start time in 'H:MM' format (e.g. '0:00'). A full day is '0:00' to '23:59'. Minutes must be one of 00, 15, 30, 45 or 59.
- `stop_time` (String) Stop time in 'H:MM' format (e.g. '23:59'). A full day is '0:00' to '23:59'. Minutes must be one of 00, 15, 30, 45 or 59.

Optional:

- `day` (String) Comma separated list of day-of-month numbers (1-31) for a specific calendar range, parallel to month. Mutually exclusive with position.
- `month` (String) Comma separated list of month numbers (1-12) for a specific calendar range, parallel to day. Mutually exclusive with position.
- `position` (String) Comma separated list of weekday numbers for a weekly recurring range, where Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7 (e.g. '1,2,3,4,5' for weekdays). Mutually exclusive with month/day.
- `range_description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).

## Import

Import is supported using the schedule name:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_firewall_schedule.example BusinessHours
```
